source_paper_title: Proximity and Flatness Bounds for Linear Integer Optimization
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Celaya_2024, title={Proximity and Flatness Bounds for Linear Integer Optimization}, volume={49}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0335}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0335}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Celaya, Marcel and Kuhlmann, Stefan and Paat, Joseph and Weismantel, Robert}, year={2024}, month=nov, pages={2446–2467} }
context:
Page 3, Introduction (discussion following Theorem 4)

It is open whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded solely by \(\Delta_n(A)\).
title: Bound lattice width of lattice-free polytopes using only determinant minor parameter
background_latex:
Let $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$ have full column rank and let $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$. Define the (rational) polyhedron
\[
P(A,b):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: Ax\le b\}.
\]
The polyhedron is called lattice-free if $P(A,b)\cap\mathbb{Z}^n=\emptyset$.

For a nonzero direction $a\in\mathbb{R}^n\setminus\{0\}$, define the width of $P(A,b)$ in direction $a$ by
\[
w_a(P(A,b)):=\max_{x\in P(A,b)} a^\top x-\min_{y\in P(A,b)} a^\top y.
\]
The lattice width is
\[
w(P(A,b)):=\min_{a\in\mathbb{Z}^n\setminus\{0\}} w_a(P(A,b)).
\]

For $k\in\{1,\dots,n\}$ define the maximal absolute $k\times k$ minor of $A$ by
\[
\Delta_k(A):=\max\{ |\det M| : M \text{ is a } k\times k \text{ submatrix of } A\}.
\]
In particular, $\Delta_n(A)$ is the maximum absolute determinant of an $n\times n$ submatrix of $A$ (which is nonzero because $A$ has full column rank $n$).
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether there exists a function $f:\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ such that for every dimension $n\ge 2$, every full-column-rank matrix $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$, and every $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$ for which $P(A,b)$ is a (full-dimensional) lattice-free polyhedron, the lattice width satisfies
\[
w(P(A,b))\le f(\Delta_n(A)).
\]
Equivalently, decide whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded above by a function depending only on $\Delta_n(A)$ (and not on $n$).
latex:
Let $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$ have full column rank and let $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$. Define the (rational) polyhedron
\[
P(A,b):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: Ax\le b\}.
\]
The polyhedron is called lattice-free if $P(A,b)\cap\mathbb{Z}^n=\emptyset$.

For a nonzero direction $a\in\mathbb{R}^n\setminus\{0\}$, define the width of $P(A,b)$ in direction $a$ by
\[
w_a(P(A,b)):=\max_{x\in P(A,b)} a^\top x-\min_{y\in P(A,b)} a^\top y.
\]
The lattice width is
\[
w(P(A,b)):=\min_{a\in\mathbb{Z}^n\setminus\{0\}} w_a(P(A,b)).
\]

For $k\in\{1,\dots,n\}$ define the maximal absolute $k\times k$ minor of $A$ by
\[
\Delta_k(A):=\max\{ |\det M| : M \text{ is a } k\times k \text{ submatrix of } A\}.
\]
In particular, $\Delta_n(A)$ is the maximum absolute determinant of an $n\times n$ submatrix of $A$ (which is nonzero because $A$ has full column rank $n$).

Determine whether there exists a function $f:\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ such that for every dimension $n\ge 2$, every full-column-rank matrix $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$, and every $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$ for which $P(A,b)$ is a (full-dimensional) lattice-free polyhedron, the lattice width satisfies
\[
w(P(A,b))\le f(\Delta_n(A)).
\]
Equivalently, decide whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded above by a function depending only on $\Delta_n(A)$ (and not on $n$).
open_problem_quote: It is open whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded solely by \(\Delta_n(A)\).
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can computationally search for families of lattice-free polytopes with fixed $\Delta_n(A)$ and large lattice width by enumerating small integer matrices and right-hand sides and checking lattice-freeness and width via MILP/LP. However, the search space grows quickly with $n,m$ and certifying worst-case growth may require guided constructions beyond brute force.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate counterexamples can be represented programmatically as integer matrices $A$ and integer vectors $b$, with fitness given by computed lattice width subject to a constraint on $\Delta_n(A)$ and lattice-freeness. The challenge is efficiently enforcing full-dimensional lattice-freeness and exploring high-dimensional instances, but evolutionary search could plausibly discover structured families with large width or suggest sharper conjectured bounds.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The open problem asks whether the lattice width \(w(P(A,b))\) of a full-dimensional lattice-free polyhedron with integral data can be bounded by a function of \(\Delta_n(A)\) alone, independent of the dimension \(n\). The source paper establishes a strong facet-direction bound: for lattice-free \(P(A,b)\) with integral \(b\) and facet-defining rows, some row \(a\) of \(A\) satisfies \(w_a(P(A,b)) < \frac{4n+2}{9}\,\Delta_n(A)-1\). This is obtained by linking flatness to proximity via a refined spindle-walking argument and a low-dimensional volume estimate (notably a planar inequality for polygons \(Q\) with \(\tau Q\subseteq Q^\circ\)). The resulting bound is linear in both \(n\) and \(\Delta_n(A)\), but does not remove the dependence on \(n\).

Dimension-independent progress is known only under additional structure. In particular, work on lattice width and Hilbert bases shows that for certain classes of lattice-free polyhedra (e.g., simplices and special pyramids) one can bound width using determinant-type parameters, and the source paper itself proves that when all full minors lie in \(\{0,\pm k,\pm 2k\}\) one gets \(w_a(P)\le \Delta_n(A)-2\), independent of \(n\). Related structural results for bimodular matrices (\(\Delta_n(A)\le 2\)) provide strong proximity/edge properties that feed into such dimension-free bounds in small-determinant regimes.

In contrast, the best general flatness bounds without arithmetic parameters remain purely dimension-dependent (currently \(O^*(n^{4/3})\) via asymptotic convex geometry). Earlier determinant-driven bounds (e.g., depending on the least common multiple of full minors) still retain dimension dependence and can be exponentially worse than a bound in terms of \(\Delta_n(A)\) alone. Overall, no general mechanism is currently known to control the minimal lattice width direction using only \(\Delta_n(A)\) without either paying a factor depending on \(n\) or imposing extra modular/structural hypotheses on the minors. Promising directions suggested by existing work include: (i) extending the dimension-free arguments from the \(\{0,\pm k,\pm 2k\}\) setting to broader minor spectra via refined Graver/Hilbert-basis height control, and (ii) developing new transference principles that convert determinant bounds into short lattice directions without invoking global flatness constants that scale with \(n\).

sources.title: On lattice width of lattice-free polyhedra and height of Hilbert bases
sources.theorem_or_result: For several structured classes of lattice-free polyhedra (including simplices and special pyramids), the lattice width can be bounded in terms of determinant-type parameters; the paper develops bounds relating lattice width/facet width to Hilbert basis height and subdeterminants, giving dimension-independent bounds in certain modular/bimodular settings.
sources.connection: Directly addresses when width bounds can depend on determinant/minor data rather than dimension; provides positive results for special classes, highlighting what is known toward bounding width by \(\Delta_n(A)\) alone and where additional structure is needed.

sources.title: On integer programming with bounded determinants
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes flatness/lattice-width-type bounds for lattice-free polyhedra in terms of the dimension and the least common multiple of all full-rank minors (a determinant-based arithmetic parameter), yielding bounds linear in dimension but potentially exponential in \(\Delta_n(A)\).
sources.connection: Provides an earlier determinant-driven width bound, but it depends on an arithmetic refinement (lcm of minors) rather than only \(\Delta_n(A)\); serves as a baseline for the open question of removing dimension dependence and reducing to \(\Delta_n(A)\) alone.

sources.title: Integer programming with bimodular matrix
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops structural/proximity properties for integer programs with bimodular constraint matrices (\(\Delta_n(A)\le 2\)), including strong proximity along edges between fractional vertices and integer points.
sources.connection: Underpins dimension-independent bounds in the special case \(\Delta_n(A)\le 2\), which is evidence toward bounding width by a function of \(\Delta_n(A)\) alone, at least for small determinants.

sources.title: Distances between nonsymmetric convex bodies and the MM*-estimate
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides asymptotic geometric analysis tools (MM* estimate) that yield the best-known general flatness bound \(O^*(n^{4/3})\) for lattice-free convex bodies.
sources.connection: Represents the state-of-the-art dimension-dependent flatness bound; contrasts with the desired determinant-only bound and indicates the geometric barrier when no arithmetic structure is assumed.

sources.title: Integer programming with a fixed number of variables
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces the fixed-dimension polynomial-time algorithm for integer programming, relying on flatness/lattice-width bounds depending on dimension.
sources.connection: Motivates why improving flatness bounds in terms of input parameters (like \(\Delta_n(A)\)) could lead to refined complexity analyses beyond fixed dimension.

sources.title: Enumerating integer points in polytopes with bounded subdeterminants
sources.theorem_or_result: Gives algorithms for enumerating integer points in polytopes when subdeterminants are bounded, leveraging determinant structure to control combinatorial complexity.
sources.connection: Algorithmic work suggesting that bounded-minor regimes allow stronger structural control; while not directly proving determinant-only width bounds, it supports the broader program of parameterizing complexity/geometry by subdeterminants.

sources.title: Proximity and flatness bounds for linear integer optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Proves a facet-direction width bound for lattice-free \(P(A,b)\) with integral \(b\): there exists a row \(a\) of \(A\) with \(w_a(P(A,b)) < \frac{4n+2}{9}\,\Delta_n(A)-1\). Also gives dimension-independent bounds in special cases where full minors lie in \(\{0,\pm k,\pm 2k\}\).
sources.connection: This is the source paper formulating the open question; it provides the best current bound in terms of \(n\) and \(\Delta_n(A)\), and shows determinant-only (dimension-free) bounds for special structured minor patterns, but leaves the general determinant-only bound open.
